subject.title: Mathematics Analysis and Approaches (AA)
subject.description:
**IB Mathematics Analysis and Approaches** is a **Group 5** subject in the **International Baccalaureate Diploma Programme (IBDP)**. It explores **number and algebra, functions, geometry and trigonometry, statistics and probability, and calculus**, with a strong focus on **theoretical understanding, formal mathematical reasoning, and analytical problem-solving**.

The course is available at **Standard Level (SL, 150 teaching hours)** and **Higher Level (HL, 240 teaching hours)**, with **first assessment in 2021**.

### Assessment at a Glance

| Assessment | Format | SL | HL | Weighting |
|------------|--------|----|----|-----------|
| **Paper 1** | Non-calculator written exam | 90 minutes | 120 minutes | 40% |
| **Paper 2** | Calculator allowed written exam | 90 minutes | 120 minutes | 40% |
| **Paper 3** | Calculator allowed problem-solving | N/A | 60 minutes | 20% (HL only) |
| **Mathematical Exploration** | Internal Assessment | 20 hours | 20 hours | 20% |

strengths: Clear investigative thread with logical development from rationale through to evaluation
Well-defined internal structure with headings, numbered subsections and a separate conclusion
Relevant figures and tables placed appropriately to support the argument
weaknesses: Over-detailed algebraic derivations occasionally interrupt flow
Lengthy quotations and step-by-step screenshots add unnecessary bulk
Minor inconsistencies in figure/table formatting and citation reduce conciseness
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: The exploration is coherent and logically developed (Band 3), with strong organization and effective signposting. However, excess detail in some sections and minor layout issues prevent a fully concise, highly polished report. A score of 3 reflects these strengths and the need to trim redundant material.

strengths: Predominantly consistent use of mathematical notation for functions, derivatives and integrals
Effective use of multiple representations including equations, tables, graphs and a 3D illustration
Good logical structure and clarity, with most derivations clearly presented
weaknesses: Occasional notation slips (missing \u201cdx\u201d, misuse of absoluteâvalue bars)
Axes labels on some graphs are too small to read
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: Mathematical language and notation are used mostly consistently, and multiple representations are effectively employed, yielding good logical structure and clear communication. Minor notation and formatting errors keep this at Band 3, warranting a score of 3.

strengths: Significant evidence of independent thinking in choosing a personalized free-throw scenario and bespoke modelling
Clear student-driven approach with self-collected data and personal narrative
Initiative shown in suggesting extensions to other sports contexts
weaknesses: Reliance on standard online solvers limits originality
Creativity and initiative are present but not sustained at an exceptional level
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: The exploration demonstrates clear personal engagement and independent thinking (Band 2), but some conventional methods and partial reliance on external tools constrain the creativity strand to Band 2, for a total of 2 marks.

strengths: Meaningful reflection comparing results to published data and modelling assumptions
Thoughtful analysis of errors (timing, air resistance) and methodological choices
Clear evaluation of outcomes with suggestions for future work
weaknesses: Critical analysis stops short of deep insight into residuals or non-linear model limitations
Evaluation is solid but not comprehensive, lacking discussion of statistical sampling
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: Reflection is present throughout with meaningful critique and clear evaluation of outcomes (Band 2). While the analysis and implications are well handled, they do not fully reach the depth and comprehensiveness of Band 3, yielding 2 marks.

strengths: Relevant application of arc length, differentiation, integration and regression aligned to the research question
Mathematics commensurate with course level, including Euler substitution and polynomial regression
Good understanding of calculus operations with mostly correct results
weaknesses: Conceptual flaws in assuming constant horizontal velocity
Minor domain errors and reliance on online integral solver weaken rigor
Some algebraic steps are dense and prone to error without intermediate validation
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: The mathematics used is appropriate and at course level, demonstrating good understanding and mostly correct methodology (Band 3). Errors and gaps in rigor prevent a higher band, so the criterion is awarded 3 marks.

strandSelections.reasoning: Arguments are set out in a logical sequence and working is generally shown.  Some derivations become algebra-heavy without commentary, yet the reader can follow the reasoning.  Overall clarity is good, meriting the âgood logical structure and clear communicationâ band.

Derivatives and integrals are mostly carried out correctly, yet conceptual flaws exist in assuming constant horizontal velocity and in the accuracy of the arc-length calculation.  These constitute minor-to-moderate errors, so understanding is good but not thorough.

The exploration employs equations, tables, graphs and a 3-D illustration.  Graphs include regression curves and labelled axes.  Although the timeâdistance scale diagram is rather small, multiple representations are effectively used, aligning with the 3-mark descriptor.

The student maintains a clear investigative thread: rationale â modelling â calculation â evaluation.  Sections follow one another logically and signposting is effective.  Minor breaks occur when long algebraic derivations are inserted without commentary, but overall the argument is easy to follow, placing the work comfortably in the âcoherent and logically developedâ band rather than merely âsome coherence.â

Using Euler substitution and linking results to literature shows some initiative, but reliance on an online integral solver and simplified time-scaling limit creativity.  Therefore only âsome creativity or initiativeâ is demonstrated.

The student analyses sources of error (manual timing, air resistance) and the validity of the constant horizontal speed assumption.  Discussion goes beyond description but stops short of a âdeep and insightfulâ critique.

Outcomes are compared to literature and suggestions for multiple trials and other sports are given, indicating a clear evaluation with some future implications.  The assessment is adequate but not comprehensive.

Reflection appears in the conclusion and evaluation sections, comparing results to published velocity ranges and commenting on modelling assumptions.  This constitutes âmeaningfulâ reflection, though depth is moderate rather than critical throughout.

Mathematical symbols for functions, derivatives and integrals are used correctly in most instances.  Minor slips occur (e.g. equality/approximation mixed, missing dx in one integral), but usage is largely consistent, placing the work in the âmostly consistentâ band.

The investigation is clearly student-driven: self-collected data, personal narrative, and bespoke modelling choices.  This meets âclear evidence of a personal approachâ, though innovations remain conventional for this topic.

Most material is relevant, but lengthy quotations of definitions, step-by-step screenshots and an over-detailed integral substitution inflate the report to 17 pages.  Repetition of background theory could be trimmed.  Hence it is âmostly relevant with some unnecessary elementsâ rather than fully concise.

Arc length, differentiation, integration and regression are all relevant to the aim and are applied appropriately.  The mathematics chosen aligns with the investigation, fulfilling the 3-mark descriptor (ârelevant mathematics used appropriatelyâ).

Choosing a personal free-throw and deciding to model arc length with Euler substitution shows significant independent thinking, but much of the procedure follows standard textbook methods or online calculators.  Evidence is stronger than âlimitedâ yet not âoutstandingâ.

Headings, numbered sub-sections and a separate conclusion give the exploration a clear internal structure (introduction, methodology, exploration, conclusion, evaluation).  Figures and tables are generally placed near the relevant text.  While algebra occasionally overruns page width, the overall organisation is solid and clearly above the âadequateâ threshold.

Use of arc-length integral with Euler substitution and polynomial regression is commensurate with HL content, though not fully exploited (e.g., numerical integration outsourced).  Hence mathematics is âcommensurate with course levelâ rather than fully or expertly applied.

annotations.comment: The title is clear and informative, effectively signaling the focus and scope of the investigation.
The personal narrative engages the reader, but the lengthy anecdote could be trimmed to improve conciseness and maintain relevance.
Citation of âAdmin, 2023â is not sufficiently authoritative. Consider using a peer-reviewed physics text or journal for kinematics definitions.
The term âquadratic formulaâ is misused when describing the projectile path; you mean quadratic function. Precise terminology is essential.
The methodology lacks detail on how GeoGebra point coordinates were converted from pixels to real-world meters. Clarify calibration procedure.
Equation (1) uses absolute-value notation around L without justification. Remove the bars or explain their necessity.
The variables U and N are described as upper and lower limits but their physical meaning (âground distance from release to hoopâ) should be reiterated here.
In Equation (2), âtotal ground distanceâ is not defined in symbols. Introduce a symbol (e.g. D) for clarity and consistency.
The symbol table (Table 1) is helpful, but the layout is cramped. Consider splitting into two columns or using smaller font to improve readability.
The domain is incorrectly stated as â0 â¤ x â¥ 3.232â. It should read â0 â¤ x â¤ 3.232â.
Regression coefficients are quoted without the RÂ² value. Including goodness-of-fit metrics would strengthen justification of the quadratic model.
Graph 1âs axes labels are too small to read clearly; ensure all graphs have legible, consistently styled axis titles and units.
Figure numbering is inconsistent: the text refers to Figure 1, but earlier it was called Graph 1. Use a single naming convention throughout.
Differentiation notation in the derivation fâ²(x)=2Ã(â0.5652x)+2.2142 is awkward. Write it as fâ²(x)=â1.1304x+2.2142 directly.
The Euler substitution step is derived from an online source but lacks academic citation. Include full reference details for rigor.
The derivation of x(u) by squaring should include a step showing cancellation of the 1.2778xÂ² terms to confirm correctness.
When calculating u-limits, the text should explicitly link each u-value to the corresponding x-limit to improve clarity.
The quotient-rule differentiation of du/dx is dense; break into smaller algebraic steps to avoid potential errors.
Relying on an online integral solver for the final arc-length integral undermines mathematical rigor. Attempt a numeric approximation or show partial analytical work.
The assumption of constant horizontal speed in scaling time to ground distance should be critically examined and justified.
Table 2âs colspan formatting makes reading column alignments difficult. Simplify the table structure or split into separate tables.
Selecting a cubic model with RÂ²â0.952 is reasonable, but discuss why residuals or non-linearities do not warrant a higher-order fit.
The bold formatting around the constant term in s(t) is inconsistent. Present mathematical expressions with uniform styling.
The velocity function is written as v(t)=28.57116t2 â 31.94248t+10.50269 without caret notation. Use v(t)=28.57116t^2â31.94248t+10.50269 for clarity.
The integration steps for computing v_avg and a_avg jump to numeric results. Include intermediate antiderivative values to validate the calculations.
The conclusion asserts that horizontal motion reduces net acceleration below g; this conflates vector components. Clarify that horizontal velocity does not alter vertical g.
Noting that a single free throw limits generalizability is a strong reflection which you could extend by proposing statistical sampling methods.
Your suggestion to apply the method to other sports demonstrates initiative and deepens the personal engagement in the exploration.
